title: AI and Generative AI as ADM Tools
description: Use of AI (including Generative AI) for processing personal data involving ADMP is within scope of the ADMP Guideline. The issued guideline frames the AI-specific items as 'amalan terbaik' (best practices) using permissive verb 'boleh menerapkan' (may apply) — softer than the PCP's mandatory framing.

guidelineStatus.issuedReference: Garis Panduan ADMP, JPDP April 2026, Section 10 (Kecerdasan Buatan)

rule: Where AI is used for ADMP (processing personal data with legal or significant effects on data subjects), the ADMP Guideline applies — including the s7 notice obligation and (where consent is the basis) s38 withdrawal of consent. The 7 AI-specific best practices (§10.2) are permissive ('boleh').
scopingNotes: Not all use of AI involves automated decision-making and profiling
AI chatbot generating responses based on patterns learned from training data, without making decisions about users, is generally outside the trigger
AI used to analyse complex datasets to identify patterns, predict outcomes, and make decisions about individuals is within the trigger

dataProtectionConcerns.description: AI development uses large amounts of data including personal and sensitive information. Users may not know how their data is processed and may struggle to exercise rights. The Right to Information addresses this.

Training data, the trained model, and output may all contain personal information. Difficult to access or correct; risk of disclosure or output influencing outcomes for individuals in response to particular prompts. The ADM Restrictions help address this.

issuedBestPractices.framing: Section 10.2 of the issued guideline lists 7 best practices the controller 'boleh menerapkan' (may apply). Permissive verb — these are not strict obligations.

items.name: Risk assessment before deployment
items.rule: Before deploying AI solutions, decide commercial objectives and weigh them against the risks of using AI in the organisation's decision-making.

items.name: Dignity, accuracy, limitations, no inappropriate use
items.rule: When using AI for profiling that significantly affects personal rights and benefits, AI must be used in a way that respects the dignity of individuals, maintains the utmost accuracy of output, recognises the limitations of AI predictions/recommendations/judgments, and considers possible disadvantages — and must not be used for inappropriate purposes.

items.name: Inform data subject of AI use
items.rule: Disclose AI use in the privacy / personal data protection notice. Notice should be scoped to be useful without being excessively long or technical.

items.name: Mitigate over-reliance / automation bias
items.rule: When developing, providing, or using an AI system or service, pay attention and take necessary countermeasures against the risk of depending too much on AI (automated bias).

items.name: Operator training
items.rule: Where the AI system is intended for autonomous decision-making, provide training or clear explanation so organisations using the AI system properly understand how it operates.

items.name: AI not the only factor
items.rule: AI must not be implemented as the only factor in policies and/or decisions concerning people. Prevent racism and avoid actions harming people.

items.name: Trained reviewers (proactive, purposeful, authoritative, competent)
items.rule: Appoint trained human reviewers who are 'proaktif, bermatlamat, berautoriti dan kompeten'. Reviewers must check the system's recommendation, not rubber-stamp it. They must have actual meaningful influence (authority and competence) to go against the automated recommendation.

noManipulation.pcpText: Do not develop, provide, or use AI systems and services to manipulate human decision-making, recognition, or emotion, or to control individuals without their awareness.
noManipulation.issuedStatus: DROPPED — not in the issued guideline. Treat as cross-jurisdictional best practice (cf. EU AI Act Article 5, NIST AI RMF GOVERN-2.1).

exampleHighImpactUseCases: Financial Services — generative AI determining creditworthiness, affecting access to loans/mortgages/credit cards
Healthcare Diagnostics — AI predicting disease likelihood, impacting insurance premiums and eligibility
Law Enforcement and Surveillance — AI identifying individuals in public spaces, impacting privacy and civil liberties
Social Benefits and Welfare — AI determining eligibility for unemployment insurance, disability benefits, welfare programs; errors or bias can wrongfully deny benefits
crossRepoLink: AI-Governance repo controls — ADMP triggers obligations under NIST AI RMF, EU AI Act parallels, ISO 42001
